chunks.text: Sanjit A. Seshia | EECS at UC Berkeley Sanjit A. Seshia: Faculty Home Page Sanjit A. Seshia Professor Cadence Founders Chair Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences Contact Information 566 Cory Hall tel: 510-643-69

Contact Information 566 Cory Hall tel: 510-643-6968 sseshia@eecs.berkeley.edu Office Hours MW 2:30-3 PM and by appointment, 566 Cory Links Personal Homepage Publications Dissertations Research Support John Li johnli@berkeley.edu Biography Sanjit A.

t John Li johnli@berkeley.edu Biography Sanjit A. Seshia is the Cadence Founders Chair Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at the University of California, Berkeley. He received an M.S. and Ph.D. in Computer Sc

eley. He received an M.S. and Ph.D. in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University, and a B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay. His research interests are in formal methods for dependable an

interests are in formal methods for dependable and secure computing, with a current focus on the areas of cyber-physical systems, computer security, machine learning, and robotics. He has made pioneering contributions to the areas of satisfiability

ring contributions to the areas of satisfiability modulo theories (SMT), SMT-based verification, and inductive program synthesis. He is co-author of a widely-used textbook on embedded, cyber-physical systems and has led the development of technologie

systems and has led the development of technologies for cyber-physical systems education based on formal methods. His awards and honors include a Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists and Engineers (PECASE), an Alfred P. Sloan Research Fello

ineers (PECASE), an Alfred P. Sloan Research Fellowship, the Frederick Emmons Terman Award for contributions to electrical engineering and computer science education, the Donald O. Pederson Best Paper Award for the IEEE Transactions on CAD, the IEEE

Award for the IEEE Transactions on CAD, the IEEE Technical Committee on Cyber-Physical Systems (TCCPS) Mid-Career Award, the Computer-Aided Verification (CAV) Award, and the Distinguished Alumnus Award from IIT Bombay. He is a Fellow of the ACM and

rd from IIT Bombay. He is a Fellow of the ACM and the IEEE. Education 2005, Ph.D., Computer Science, Carnegie Mellon University 2000, M.S., Computer Science, Carnegie Mellon University 1998, B.Tech., Computer Science and Engineering, Indian Institute

Computer Science and Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay Research Areas Cyber-Physical Systems and Design Automation (CPSDA) Programming Systems (PS) Artificial Intelligence (AI) Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR) Securi
